subject: This week: subagents are a new unit
preview_text: A short field note on how I started measuring LLM work in subagent-units rather than tokens, plus what I'm reading this week.
html_body:
Hi friends,

This week I rewrote ~6000 lines of agent glue into 12 subagents. Wall-clock time fell from 18 min to 4 min, but the real lesson wasn't speed — it was that I had been measuring LLM work in the wrong unit all along.

The piece

Full write-up here: {article_title}. Short version: subagent-count is a better accounting unit than token-count for any task with > 2 passes of reasoning.

Concrete example in the post: the same PR review prompt costs 3 subagent-units regardless of repo size, but the token cost ranges 5x. If you budget by tokens you over-quote small repos and under-quote big ones.

Closing

If this resonates, hit reply — I want to hear what unit you're using. Forwarding is welcome.

You're getting this because you subscribed at my site. Unsubscribe.
